title: Build wireless channel simulator
description: Python module simulating LoRa radio propagation. Features: (1) Distance-based path loss model, (2) SF/BW impact on range and data rate, (3) Packet collision detection (simultaneous TX), (4) Configurable packet loss/BER, (5) Propagation delay, (6) Duty cycle tracking per node, (7) SNR/RSSI calculation. Should support multiple nodes in a topology.

title: Validate STM32WL memory budget
description: Build minimal Zephyr+IPv6+CoAP on nucleo_wl55jc. Run west build -t ram_report and rom_report. Target: <50KB RAM, <220KB flash. If exceeded, evaluate RIOT OS fallback. This is a go/no-go gate for Zephyr on STM32WL.

title: Spec: Design 6LoWPAN over LoRa adaptation layer
description: Listed as 'open question' but foundational. LoRa is not 802.15.4 — Zephyr's 6LoWPAN assumes 802.15.4 MAC semantics (ACKs, CSMA-CA timing, frame formats). Need concrete adaptation layer design covering: frame format mapping, ACK handling, timing parameters, integration with SCHC.
close_reason: Resolved: Use SCHC (RFC 8724) instead of 6LoWPAN IPHC. No 802.15.4 anywhere. Stack: IPv6 → SCHC → LICHEN Link Layer → LoRa PHY. Zephyr requires custom L2. Updated spec/03-adaptation.md.

title: Spec: Define time synchronization mechanism
description: Time sync is listed as 'open question' but is critical for: replay protection (SeqNum wrapping), SenML timestamps, message TTL expiration, scheduled check-ins, OSCORE sequence numbers. Options mentioned: NTP over CoAP, GPS, DIO piggyback. Need to pick one and specify protocol.
close_reason: Resolved: Hierarchical time sources (GPS > gpsd > NTS > Roughtime > mesh peer > none). Stratum propagated via DIO Time Option. Constrained nodes gracefully degrade to monotonic counters. Updated spec/09-packets-timing.md.

title: Spec: Define truncated Ed25519 algorithm
description: The spec says 'we use a deterministic scheme where the second half is derived from the first' for truncated Ed25519 signatures but provides no concrete algorithm. This is non-standard cryptography with no security analysis. Need to either: (1) specify exact derivation algorithm with security proof, or (2) switch to standard ECDSA approach mentioned in 8.4. Violates 'no hand-rolled crypto' principle.
close_reason: Resolved: Use Schnorr (e₁₂₈, s) variant - 48 bytes, well-known construction, 128-bit security. Added relay-mutable vs signed field separation and signature caching. Updated spec/06-security.md.

title: Implement Zephyr native_sim radio driver for simulator
description:
Create a Zephyr radio driver for native_sim target that connects to our LoRa simulator over TCP.

This allows running the full Zephyr LICHEN stack (SCHC, RPL, CoAP) as a Linux process, with radio operations handled by our simulator.

Implementation:
1. Create drivers/lora/lora_sim.c - Zephyr LoRa driver
2. Implement Zephyr LoRa API (lora_config, lora_send, lora_recv)
3. TCP client connecting to simulator port 4444
4. Wire protocol: same binary protocol as Python SimRadio client
5. Kconfig options: CONFIG_LORA_SIM, CONFIG_LORA_SIM_HOST, CONFIG_LORA_SIM_PORT

Driver behavior:
- On init: TCP connect, send REGISTER with node_id from devicetree/Kconfig
- lora_send(): encode TX message, block until TX_DONE/TX_FAIL
- lora_recv(): encode RX message with timeout, block until RX_OK/RX_TIMEOUT
- On deinit: close TCP connection

Benefits:
- Native speed (no QEMU)
- Full debugging (gdb, valgrind, asan, coverage)
- CI-friendly (Linux binary)
- Same simulator as Python/Rust implementations

Depends on simulator being operational and wire protocol finalized.

Reference: Zephyr LoRa API - https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/hardware/peripherals/lora.html

title: Evaluate libschc for SCHC implementation
description:
Evaluate libschc (imec-idlab/libschc) for use in LICHEN.

Tasks:
1. Clone and review libschc code structure
2. Check RFC 8724 coverage - what's implemented vs missing
3. Review fragmentation support (ACK-on-Error mode we need)
4. Assess memory footprint and malloc-free operation
5. Test with our SCHC rules (Appendix A of spec)
6. Compare with CSCHC (Orange, MIT license) as alternative

Integration paths to evaluate:
- Python: Can we use libschc via cffi/ctypes, or reimplement in pure Python?
- Zephyr: Port as module (RIOT OS has done this already)
- Rust: FFI bindings or reimplement in Rust

Deliverables:
- Summary of libschc capabilities and gaps
- Recommendation: use libschc, use CSCHC, or write our own
- If using libschc: integration plan for Python simulator and Zephyr

References:
- https://github.com/imec-idlab/libschc
- https://github.com/quentinlampin/cschc
- https://doc.riot-os.org/group__pkg__libschc.html (RIOT integration)

title: Integration test: node mobility
description: Test: two nodes A and B, initially 100m apart (good link). A transmits, B receives. Move B to 5000m via REST API. A transmits, B cannot receive (out of range or very weak). Move B back to 200m. A transmits, B receives again. Verify RSSI changes match distance.

title: Integration test: chaos monkey operations
description: Test: set up 3-node network, verify baseline connectivity. Apply DropRule: verify packets dropped. Apply PartitionRule: verify cross-partition packets blocked. Apply DegradeRule: verify RSSI reduced. Apply JammerRule: verify nodes in radius can't communicate. Clear chaos, verify connectivity restored. Use REST API to apply/clear rules.

title: Integration test: collision and capture effect
description: Test: three nodes A, B, C. A and B transmit simultaneously to C. Case 1: equal power, both lost. Case 2: A is 10dB stronger, A wins (capture effect). Case 3: A is 4dB stronger, both lost (below 6dB threshold). Verify packet delivery matches expected behavior.

title: Integration test: two-node TX/RX
description: Create tests/sim/test_integration.py. Test: start simulator server, connect two SimRadio clients (nodes A and B at 100m apart), A transmits, B receives with correct RSSI/SNR. Verify: packet delivered, RSSI matches propagation model, airtime correct. Use pytest-asyncio. Fixture to start/stop simulator server.

title: Implement DutyCycleTracker
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/duty_cycle.py. DutyCycleTracker class: __init__(limit_percent=1.0, window_seconds=3600). Methods: record_tx(airtime_us, time_us), can_transmit(airtime_us, time_us) -> bool, get_usage(time_us) -> float (0.0-1.0). Sliding window: track TX times, drop entries older than window. EU 868MHz limit: 1% duty cycle. Integration: TCP server checks before allowing TX, returns TX_FAIL if exceeded. Unit tests for boundary conditions.

title: Implement simulator main entry point
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/server.py (main module). SimulatorServer class: __init__(node_port=4444, api_port=4445), async start(), async stop(). Starts both TCP node server and HTTP REST API. Manages SimulationRegistry (dict of active simulations). CLI entry point: python -m lichen.sim --node-port 4444 --api-port 4445 --log-level INFO. Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. Structlog for logging.

title: Implement SimRadio client
description: Create python/src/lichen/radio/sim_client.py. SimRadio class implementing Radio protocol. __init__(host, port, sim_id, node_id, position). async connect(): open TCP, send REGISTER, await OK. async transmit(payload) -> bool: send TX, await TX_DONE/TX_FAIL. async receive(timeout_ms) -> tuple | None: send RX, await RX_OK/RX_TIMEOUT. async get_time() -> int: send TIME, await TIME_OK. async close(): close TCP connection. Context manager support (__aenter__, __aexit__). Reconnection logic on disconnect. Unit tests using mock TCP server.

title: Implement pcapng packet capture
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/pcap.py. PcapngWriter class: __init__(path), write_shb() (Section Header Block), write_idb() (Interface Description Block with DLT_USER0, name='lichen-lora', custom options for SF/BW/CR), write_epb(timestamp_us, data, rssi, snr, src_node, dst_node) (Enhanced Packet Block with custom options). Use struct for binary packing. Follow pcapng spec (IETF draft-tuexen-opsawg-pcapng). Integration with Medium to capture all TX/RX events. Unit test: write file, verify with tshark -r.

title: Implement REST control API
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/api.py using Starlette. Endpoints: POST /sim (create simulation), DELETE /sim/{id}, GET /sim/{id} (status), POST /sim/{id}/tick (advance time). Node management: POST /sim/{id}/node, DELETE /sim/{id}/node/{nid}, PATCH /sim/{id}/node/{nid} (move). Chaos: POST /sim/{id}/chaos/drop, /partition, /degrade, /jam, DELETE /sim/{id}/chaos. Observe: GET /sim/{id}/packets, GET /sim/{id}/topology, WebSocket /sim/{id}/stream for live events. JSON request/response. Return appropriate HTTP status codes.

title: Implement TCP node server
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/node_server.py. asyncio TCP server on port 4444. On connect: read REGISTER message, create/lookup Simulation by sim_id, add node, send OK/ERR. Handle TX: validate, create Transmission in medium, schedule TxEndEvent, send TX_DONE with airtime. Handle RX: set node state to RX_WAIT, create RxTimeoutEvent, await packet delivery or timeout, send RX_OK or RX_TIMEOUT. Handle TIME: return current_time_us. Track connection -> node_id mapping. Clean up on disconnect.

title: Implement chaos rules framework
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/chaos.py. ChaosRule ABC with matches(tx, rx_node) -> bool and apply(tx) -> Transmission | None. Concrete rules: DropRule(node_id, direction='tx|rx|both', count), PartitionRule(groups: list[set[str]]), DegradeRule(node_id, rssi_penalty_db), JammerRule(x, y, z, radius_m, power_dbm), LatencyRule(node_id, added_us). ChaosEngine class: add_rule(rule), remove_rule(rule_id), clear(), apply_all(tx, rx_node) -> Transmission | None. Unit tests for each rule type.

title: Define wire protocol encoding/decoding
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/protocol.py. Binary protocol, little-endian. Message types: REGISTER(0x01), OK(0x00), ERR(0xFF), TX(0x10), TX_DONE(0x11), TX_FAIL(0x12), RX(0x20), RX_OK(0x21), RX_TIMEOUT(0x22), TIME(0x30), TIME_OK(0x31). Functions: encode_register(sim_id, node_id, x, y, z) -> bytes, decode_register(data) -> tuple, encode_tx(payload) -> bytes, etc. Use struct module. Comprehensive round-trip unit tests for each message type.

title: Implement Simulation class
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/simulation.py. Simulation class: id (str), nodes (dict[str, SimNode]), medium (Medium), event_queue (EventQueue), current_time_us (int), time_mode enum (BARRIER_SYNC, REALTIME). Methods: add_node(id, x, y, z) -> SimNode, remove_node(id), get_node(id), advance_to(time_us), process_next_event(), maybe_advance_time() for barrier sync (advances when all nodes in RX_WAIT). Barrier sync: deterministic, waits for all nodes to block before advancing. Realtime: advances with wall clock.

title: Implement SimNode state tracking
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/node.py. SimNode dataclass: id (str), position (tuple[float,float,float]), tx_power_dbm (default 22), state enum (IDLE, TX, RX_WAIT), pending_rx_future (for async wakeup), connected (bool), last_seen_time_us. Methods: set_position(x,y,z), disconnect(), is_online(). Position is mutable for mobility support.

title: Implement Medium class
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/medium.py. Tracks active transmissions. Methods: start_tx(node_id, payload, tx_power) -> Transmission, get_rx_candidates(node_id, position, time) -> list of (Transmission, rssi, snr). Uses PropagationModel for path loss. Collision detection: find overlapping TXs. Capture effect: if strongest signal is 6+ dB above second strongest, it wins; otherwise all colliding packets lost. Configurable noise_floor_dbm (default -120). Unit tests for: single TX delivery, collision with both lost, capture effect winner.

title: Implement Transmission and airtime calculation
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/transmission.py. Transmission dataclass: id (uuid), source_node_id, payload (bytes), tx_power_dbm, start_time_us, end_time_us, frequency_hz. Function airtime_us(payload_len: int) -> int using fixed SF10/125kHz/CR4-5 parameters. Formula: T = (2^SF / BW) * (preamble + 4.25 + 8 + max(ceil((8*PL - 4*SF + 28 + 16) / (4*SF)) * CR, 0)). Preamble=8 symbols. Unit tests with known payload sizes.

title: Implement event queue system
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/events.py. Priority queue sorted by time. Event base class with time field. Subclasses: TxStartEvent, TxEndEvent, RxTimeoutEvent. EventQueue class: push(event), pop() -> Event, peek() -> Event | None, is_empty() -> bool. All times in microseconds (int). Unit tests for ordering, ties broken by insertion order.

title: Implement propagation model
description:
Create python/src/lichen/sim/propagation.py. Log-distance path loss: PL = PL₀ + 10·n·log₁₀(d/d₀). Default PL₀=-127.41dBm at d₀=1m for 915MHz. Configurable exponent n (2.0=free space, 2.7=urban, 3.5=indoor).

SF sensitivity thresholds from LoRaSim (Bor et al., 2016, 'Do LoRa Low-Power Wide-Area Networks Scale?'):
  SF7: -123 dBm, SF8: -126 dBm, SF9: -129 dBm, SF10: -132 dBm, SF11: -134.5 dBm, SF12: -137 dBm
  (at 125kHz BW)

Capture effect threshold: 6 dB (stronger signal wins if delta >= 6dB, per LoRaSim).

We use SF10 fixed, so threshold is -132 dBm.

Class PropagationModel with:
- path_loss(distance_m) -> float (dB)
- snr(tx_power_dbm, distance_m, noise_floor_dbm) -> float
- can_decode(snr) -> bool (snr > sensitivity - noise_floor)

Unit tests with known distances. Cite LoRaSim paper in docstring.

title: Define Radio ABC/Protocol
description: Create python/src/lichen/radio/base.py with Radio Protocol (typing.Protocol). Methods: async transmit(payload: bytes) -> bool, async receive(timeout_ms: int) -> tuple[bytes, int, int] | None (payload, rssi, snr), configure(freq_hz: int, tx_power_dbm: int) -> None. This is the interface all radio implementations must satisfy.

title: Create simulator Python project structure
description: Set up python/src/lichen/sim/ directory structure with pyproject.toml. Dependencies: anyio, starlette, uvicorn, pynacl, structlog. Dev deps: pytest, pytest-asyncio, httpx. Create empty __init__.py files for: sim/, radio/, link/. This is the foundation for all simulator work.

title: Create Python project structure
description: Set up python/ directory with: pyproject.toml, src/lichen/, tests/, uv or poetry for deps. Dependencies: pynacl, aiocoap, asyncio. Type hints throughout. pytest for testing.

title: Build simulation harness
description: Python simulation runner. Features: (1) Topology definition (node positions, links), (2) Event-driven simulation loop, (3) Packet capture/logging, (4) Metrics collection (latency, delivery rate, collisions), (5) Visualization of DODAG formation. Support for reproducible runs (seeded RNG).

title: Integrate aiocoap for CoAP
description: Use aiocoap library for CoAP client/server. Integrate with LICHEN stack (SCHC compression, link layer). Implement basic resources: /.well-known/core, /status. Test request/response over simulated multi-hop mesh.

title: Implement RPL routing in Python
description: Python implementation of RFC 6550 RPL. DIO/DIS/DAO/DAO-ACK messages. Trickle timer. MRHOF objective function with ETX. DODAG formation. Non-storing mode with source routing. Test with 5+ node topology.

title: Implement minimal IPv6/6LoWPAN in Python
description: Python IPv6 packet construction/parsing. 6LoWPAN IPHC compression. Address handling: link-local, ULA, IID derivation from EUI-64. ICMPv6 echo. Integrate with SCHC layer.

title: Implement SCHC compression in Python
description: Python implementation of RFC 8724 SCHC. Implement rules 0-4 from spec Appendix A. Compression and decompression. ACK-on-Error fragmentation. Generate test vectors for each rule.

title: Implement link layer in Python
description: Python implementation of spec section 4: frame format (Length+LLSec+SeqNum+DstAddr+Payload+Sig), Ed25519 signatures (use PyNaCl), replay protection with sliding window. Generate test vectors.

title: Implement simulated radio interface
description: Python Radio class matching the trait from spec. Methods: configure(), transmit(), receive(), cad(), rssi(), snr(). Backed by wireless channel simulator. Async/event-driven design.

title: Python prototype validates protocol design
description: Gate for moving to embedded implementation. Python prototype must demonstrate: SCHC compression works, RPL DODAG forms in simulation, CoAP messaging works end-to-end, test vectors generated for Rust/C.

title: Implement replay protection
description: 16-bit sequence number with 32-entry sliding window per sender. Track per-sender SeqNum state. Reject if SeqNum <= last_seen (accounting for window).

title: Implement link layer frame format
description: Per spec section 4: Length(1B) + LLSec(1B) + SeqNum(2B) + DstAddr(2-8B) + Payload + MIC(4-8B). Implement encoding/decoding in both Rust and C. Create test vectors.

title: Implement Radio abstraction trait
description: Create Radio trait with: configure(), transmit(), receive(), cad(), rssi(), snr(). Implement for sx126x, sx127x, and simulated backends. Per spec section 3.

title: Set up Zephyr west workspace
description: Create zephyr/ directory with west.yml manifest. Set up CMakeLists.txt, Kconfig. Create board overlays for heltec_lora32_v3, rak4631, tbeam_supreme, nucleo_wl55jc. Verify west build succeeds for each board.

title: Create Rust workspace with no_std crates
description: Set up rust/ directory with Cargo.toml workspace. Create stub crates: lichen-core, lichen-link, lichen-schc, lichen-coap, lichen-senml, lichen-apps, lichen-node, lichen-gateway, lichen-sim. All core crates should be no_std by default.

title: Phase 4: RPL Routing
description: Mesh formation with MRHOF objective function. Exit criteria: 5-node mesh forms DODAG, upward and downward routing work, parent switching on link failure.

title: Phase 3: IPv6 and 6LoWPAN
description: Full IPv6 with layered addressing. Exit criteria: nodes can ping (link-local), ULA addresses work, isolated mesh self-organizes.

title: Phase 2: SCHC Compression
description: RFC 8724 SCHC for IPv6/UDP/CoAP compression. Exit criteria: all rules implemented and tested, fragmentation working, Rust and C produce identical output.

title: Phase 1: Physical & Link Layer
description: Transmit and receive authenticated frames over LoRa. Exit criteria: two nodes exchange authenticated frames, replay attack blocked, test vectors for link layer.

title: Phase 0: Python Prototype + Simulation
description: Build Python prototype with simulated wireless to validate protocol design before embedded implementation. Exit criteria: multi-node simulation runs, SCHC compression validated, RPL DODAG forms, CoAP messaging works end-to-end over simulated LoRa links.

title: Spec: Address 16-bit sequence number wrapping
description: 16-bit SeqNum with 32-entry window is weak. High-traffic nodes wrap in ~65K packets. After wrap, delayed replays become possible. Need: (1) specify behavior on wrap, (2) add epoch counter or timestamp component, (3) define state recovery after reboot. Consider 32-bit SeqNum or hybrid approach.

title: Spec: Add SOS authentication and rate limiting
description: Emergency/SOS section specifies 're-broadcast once (TTL-limited)' but no authentication. Attack vector: flood network with fake SOS messages causing DoS. Need: (1) require Ed25519 signature on SOS, (2) specify rate limiting (e.g., 1 SOS per node per hour), (3) define reputation/blacklist for SOS abusers.

title: Spec: Define OSCORE key derivation from Ed25519
description: TOFU trust model binds Ed25519 pubkeys to nodes, but OSCORE requires symmetric master secrets. How are OSCORE contexts derived from Ed25519 keypairs? Options: ECDH-style key agreement, HKDF from shared secret, pre-shared context. Must specify concrete algorithm for trust model to work end-to-end.
close_reason: Added EDHOC (RFC 9528) for OSCORE key derivation in spec/06-security.md section 8.8

title: Write draft-lichen-link-01.md
description: IETF-style Internet-Draft for link layer. Cover: frame format, LLSec byte, addressing modes, sequence numbers, Ed25519 truncated signatures. Use RFC 2119 keywords.

title: Create test vector framework
description: Set up test/vectors/ with JSON schema for test vectors. Create initial vectors for: link layer frames, SCHC compression. Both Rust and C implementations must validate against these vectors.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI
description: Create .github/workflows/ with: Rust build/test/clippy/fmt, Zephyr build for all target boards, test vector validation. CI should run on push and PR.

title: Define shared constants
description: Create shared header/module with: frequencies per region, sync word 0x34, CoAP ports (5683, 5684), MQTT-SN port (10883), SCHC rule IDs, RPL parameters. Must be usable from both Rust and C.

title: Phase 9: Border Router
description: Full 6LBR connecting mesh to internet. Exit criteria: internet host can ping mesh node, mesh node can reach internet, Resource Directory operational.

title: Phase 8: Applications
description: Tactical radio features: messaging, position sharing, waypoints, SOS, presence, check-in, range testing, groups. Exit criteria: all P0 applications working, SOS priority handling works, group messaging works.

title: Phase 7: SenML Sensors
description: RFC 8428 SenML for all sensor data. Exit criteria: all sensor profiles implemented, observable resources work, position beaconing works.

title: Phase 6: Local Client Interface
description: IPv6+CoAP interface for phone apps and local clients. Exit criteria: SLIP framing works over serial, BLE UART works, CoAP resources implemented, client can address mesh nodes through local node.

title: Phase 5: Security Layer
description: TOFU trust model, OSCORE encryption. Exit criteria: TOFU key exchange works, OSCORE-protected CoAP works, key pinning with change detection.

title: Spec: Add store-and-forward limits and back-pressure
description: Store-and-forward has 'implementation-defined' limits — no interop guarantees. Attack: exhaust node storage with messages to offline targets. Need: mandatory minimum/maximum limits, back-pressure signaling (e.g., 5.03 Service Unavailable), memory reservation policy.
close_reason: Added storage limits (8-64 msgs, 1-16KB), FIFO+fairness eviction, 5.03 back-pressure. spec/12-apps.md section 18.1.4

title: Spec: Harden isolated mesh root election
description: 'Lowest EUI-64 wins' is deterministic but exploitable. Attacker can manufacture nodes with low EUI-64. No mechanism to demote misbehaving roots. No re-election on root failure. Consider: randomized election with proof-of-work, reputation system, election timeout, demotion protocol.
close_reason: Added root failure detection (3×Imax timeout), re-election protocol, and demotion voting (>50% threshold). spec/04-network.md

title: Spec: Define short address collision handling
description: Short address assignment says 'Hash lower 16 bits, check for collision' but no collision detection mechanism for lossy mesh. DAD over LoRa is unreliable. Race conditions when multiple nodes join. Need: concrete DAD protocol with timeouts, collision resolution, coordinator-assisted fallback.
close_reason: Added section 4.5: coordinator assigns when present, hash+DAD when isolated, signature mismatch detects collisions. spec/02-physical-link.md

title: Spec: Add congestion control for CoAP
description: No congestion control specified. CoAP CON retries over duty-cycle-limited links can cause: cascading retry storms, EU 868MHz duty cycle violations, network collapse under load. Need: adaptive retry backoff, duty cycle awareness, load shedding policy.
close_reason: Added CoAP params for LoRa (15s ACK_TIMEOUT, 2 retries), duty cycle tracking with congestion levels, 5.03 load shedding, priority queue. spec/07-transport-app.md section 10.1.1-10.1.2

title: Spec: Resolve RPL secure mode contradiction
description: Design principle: 'No pre-shared network keys'. Section 8.7: 'Recommended: Preinstalled mode with network-wide key'. These contradict. Either: (1) drop RPL secure mode recommendation, (2) use Authenticated mode with per-node keys, (3) clarify that control plane PSK is acceptable while data plane is per-peer.
close_reason: Resolved: link-layer sigs are baseline, RPL PSK is optional defense-in-depth. Updated spec/06-security.md section 8.9.

title: Spec: Define group membership protocol
description: Groups extensively documented for messaging/encryption but no join/leave protocol. Missing: membership synchronization, authorization model, key distribution mechanism (currently 'out-of-band'). Need concrete protocol for group CRUD operations and member management.
close_reason: Added section 18.8.2: roles (owner/admin/member), invitations, key distribution via pairwise OSCORE, leave/remove, rekeying on removal. spec/12-apps.md

title: Spec: Define OTA firmware update mechanism
description: No firmware update mechanism specified. Critical for IoT security — devices need patches. Consider: CoAP block-wise transfer for images, SUIT manifest (RFC 9019), secure boot verification, rollback protection. This is essential for production deployment.

title: Spec: Add SCHC rule versioning
description: SCHC rules are pre-provisioned with no versioning scheme. Problems: (1) no fallback when rules mismatch, (2) firmware updates could silently break interop, (3) no error detection for rule mismatch. Need: rule version field in packets, negotiation/fallback mechanism, or minimum version advertisement in DIO.
close_reason: Added section 5.7: DIO advertises rule version, Rule 255 reserved for uncompressed fallback. spec/03-adaptation.md

title: Phase 10: Tooling & Polish
description: lichen-craft CLI, Wireshark dissector, network simulator, web dashboard. Exit criteria: all tools functional, documentation complete, example applications working.

title: Spec: Complete position privacy model
description: 'group' privacy mode says 'query requires auth' but no mechanism defined. Beacons still reveal presence to passive eavesdroppers. Group membership undefined. Need: auth protocol for position queries, beacon encryption option, group membership definition.
close_reason: Added query auth (OSCORE required), group beacon encryption, private mode whitelist, documented presence vs location limitation. spec/12-apps.md section 18.2.4

title: Spec: Address link-layer metadata exposure
description: Spec acknowledges 'Metadata visible: Link-layer headers unencrypted' but doesn't mitigate. Enables: traffic analysis, node tracking via IID/EUI-64, network topology discovery by passive adversaries. Consider: IID rotation, traffic padding, dummy messages for cover traffic.
close_reason: Not a concern per threat model: auth-only, not concealment. Running in the clear is intentional.

title: Spec: Define CoAP block-wise vs SCHC fragmentation interaction
description: Both CoAP block-wise (RFC 7959) and SCHC (RFC 8724) fragment large payloads. Interaction unspecified: which layer fragments first? How do reassembly failures propagate? What's the overhead when both active? Need clear layering rules and failure handling.
close_reason: CoAP block-wise NOT RECOMMENDED. SCHC handles all fragmentation. Large transfers use app-level chunking. spec/07-transport-app.md section 10.3
